reasoning_model_1: The model fails the fundamental criterion of Content Accuracy. The user requested the model to read the specific sentence: 'Every word, must be read clearly.' However, the model hallucinates and paraphrases the text, saying 'Every word is spoken with care' (Timestamp: 0:04-0:08). Although the pace of this incorrect sentence is slow, the content is clearly inconsistent with the script provided in the prompt. According to the scoring standard, if voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script, the score is 0. Additionally, the model includes conversational filler ('Of course. Here is the sentence...') which was not requested.
reasoning_model_2: The model performs perfectly regarding Content Accuracy, reading the text 'Every word must be read clearly' exactly as requested without any conversational filler or hallucinations. Regarding the 'Pace' dimension, the model executes the instruction 'very slow pace' exceptionally well. It significantly elongates the duration of words (e.g., 'Every' at 0:00-0:02, 'Clearly' at 0:06-0:08) and places distinct, deliberate pauses between words while maintaining natural prosody. The resulting speech is extremely slow yet remains fluent and natural, perfectly embodying the user's command.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed to read the correct text script, substituting the target sentence 'Every word, must be read clearly' with 'Every word is spoken with care.' This is a critical failure in content accuracy. Model 2, in contrast, read the correct text and applied the 'very slow pace' feature effectively, demonstrating a much more deliberate and elongated delivery than Model 1. Model 2 followed all instructions, while Model 1 failed the basic text-to-speech task.
